The '''dragon hasta''' is a one-handed [[melee]] [[weapon]] that requires 60 [[Attack]] and completion of the [[Barbarian Training#Smithing|Smithing]] section of the [[Barbarian Training]] [[miniquest]] to be wielded. Like [[spears]], the dragon hasta can be [[Poison#Weapon poisoning|poisoned]].

Dragon hastae are obtained as loot from the [[brimstone chest]], which requires a [[brimstone key]] to open. The dragon hasta is obtained in [[Broken dragon hasta|broken]] form and can be repaired for free by showing it to [[Otto Godblessed]] (the repair does '''not''' require Barbarian Smithing).

Like most hastae, it provides negative defensive bonuses, but when equipped with a shield, the combined defence bonuses will be higher than those provided by the [[dragon spear]].

As with most [[dragon equipment]], the dragon hasta '''cannot''' be made from raw materials using the [[Smithing]] skill.

==Special attack==
[[File:Unleash.gif|frame|A player performs the dragon hasta's special attack, ''Unleash''.]]
The dragon hasta has a [[special attack]] called ''Unleash'', which drains the player's special attack bar and attacks with a 5% boost in accuracy and a 2.5% boost in damage for every 5% of special attack energy used. Notably, it ignores the 40% damage reduction from [[Protect from Melee]] if the opponent has the prayer active.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1581996752193789952|date=17 October 2022|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/iP4xr|archivedate=21 October 2022|quote=It [Unleash] ignores the damage reduction effect [of Protect from Melee], unlike most attacks.}} This special rolls off of the opponents stab defence regardless of which mode it is in.

For example, a player with 100% special attack energy who performs the special attack will have 100% increased accuracy (meaning the accuracy is doubled) and a damage boost of 50%.

==Changes==
{{Subject changes header}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 19 March 2025
|update = Clan Events Improvements
|change = The dragon hasta [[special attack]] will now work correctly in [[PvP]] when the player's opponent is not using the [[Protect from Melee]] [[protection prayer]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 7 August 2024
|update = Poll 82 Changes
|change = The red glow from the special attack animation has been updated to use a unique one rather than reuse that of the [[dragon sword]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 7 July 2021
|update = Small Changes & Tweaks
|poll =
|change = The position of the weapon and its variants on female players has been adjusted so that they appear to be equipped properly.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 14 February 2019
|update = Boss Heads and Chambers of Xeric
|change = The examine text of the dragon hasta(kp) variant was changed. The text was changed from "A poison-tipped, one-handed dragon hasta." to "A karambwan poison-tipped, one-handed dragon hasta." for consistency with other karambwan poison-tipped hastae.
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 24 January 2019
|update = Boxing Mechanics and Lots of Fixes
|change = The Karambwan poison variant is no longer tradeable. [[Noted]] variants of the dragon hasta(kp) that remain in the player's possession were converted into the item [[Fake dragon hasta(kp)]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 17 January 2019
|update = Kebos Graphical Changes and Other Fixes
|change = Item [[value]] decreased from 100,000 to 62,400 [[coins]].
}}
{{Subject changes
|date = 14 January 2019
|update = Konar Loot Table Balancing
|type = hotfix
|change = The Dragon hasta no longer deals full damage to the [[Corporeal Beast]].{{CiteTwitter|author=Old School RuneScape|url=https://twitter.com/OldSchoolRS/status/1084834960543043585|date=14 January 2019|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/103571|archivedate=25 May 2020}}
}}
